Fuelight 360: Synthetic Persona Review

Stress-testing the Bulk Edit Side Panel (CXP & Market) through 5 grounded personas before scheduling user-facing research. Identifying gaps in assumptions, surfacing design problems, and defining clear next steps for moderated and unmoderated testing.

Assessment date: July 16, 2026

Initial Ask

Before investing in moderated or unmoderated user testing, stress-test the Bulk Edit Side Panel (CXP & Market) through synthetic personas grounded in real Fuelight discovery interviews. Identify what problems are worth solving and what's missing — so sessions are focused on validating, not discovering.

Feature: Quick Edit (Bulk Edit Side Panel) Testing: Moderated sessions in progress · Unmoderated TBD Method: Tier 1 — Synthetic (AI persona review, internal)

The Core Design Gap

Thesis The Bulk Edit panel presents data but not decisions. Every persona — regardless of seniority, literacy, or workflow — needs the same thing the tool doesn't provide: context that turns a number into a judgment call. "Is this good? Should I act? Can I defend it?"

The tool solves the mechanical problem (change numbers in bulk) without solving the decision problem (why these numbers, what does it mean, and can I defend it). It's built for one user type: someone who already knows exactly what to change. Even that user finds it slow.

Method

This assessment follows the Tier 1: Synthetic research method — an internal practice using AI persona agents grounded in real Fuelight discovery quotes to stress-test designs before investing in user-facing sessions.

Steps Taken

  1. Feature audit — Read full source (~5,700 lines each) of Side Panel CXP and Market to document every control, interaction, and post-apply behavior
  2. Persona profiles — Extracted all 5 personas from the Persona Lab: roles, decision routines, pain points, data literacy, and Fuelight expectations
  3. Framework mapping — Grouped personas into 3 tiers: Executive Decision Makers, Operational Managers, Analytical Power Users
  4. Stress test — Ran each persona through the feature: "What would they do? What confuses them? What's missing?"
  5. Cross-reference — Built gap matrix identifying shared themes vs. persona-specific issues
  6. Synthesis — Distilled into patterns, challenges, and prioritised design principles

Personas Used

Persona Role Tier Evaluation Lens
DanielVP, Franchise Operations Senior decision-maker, ABP owner, IC submitter Executive Can I trust and defend this output in 60 seconds?
CamilaSr. Director, Finance IC gatekeeper, E-rate owner, adoption validator Executive Can I audit the assumptions? Is this financially defensible?
MarcoDirector, Franchise Operations Core power user, DFR owner, runs tool weekly Operational Can I act on this fast enough before the window closes?
SarahDirector, Media / IMX Highest data literacy, methodology hawk Analytical Is the math sound? Does it respect how media actually works?
ElenaSr. Director, Strategy LRP/ABP owner, portfolio trade-offs Analytical Does this change the forward plan or just explain the past?

Overarching Themes

Three themes emerge across all five personas, regardless of archetype. These are the structural problems the tool doesn't solve today.

A

The tool edits numbers but never explains what they mean

Every persona — from the VP who needs a one-liner for IC, to the analyst who needs methodology — hits the same wall: the panel shows what changed but never why it matters. The narrative layer is completely absent.

B

Nothing is traceable, auditable, or defensible

No rationale is captured, no benchmark is referenced, no confidence is signaled. Users can't defend a rule they applied, approve one someone else applied, or understand why a number moved. Trust requires transparency — and transparency is zero.

C

The tool exists in a single moment, not across a workflow

Scenarios vanish on refresh. Rules can't be reused. Outputs can't be shared. The panel is a disposable scratch pad in a world where decisions need persistence, comparison, and collaboration across planning cycles.

What Good Looks Like

1

Every number needs a reference point

Show vs. target, vs. last year, vs. plan — never just the raw value.

2

Every edit needs a rationale

Capture why, not just what. Even a one-line text field transforms auditability.

3

The operator and the decision-maker see the same data differently

Build one table, two modes: edit mode (Marco) and review mode (Daniel/Camila).

4

Speed and rigour are sequential, not competing

Fast to apply (Marco), auditable after the fact (Camila).

Cross-Persona Assessment

Three questions per persona, grouped by archetype: what they'd try to do, what frustrates them, and what's missing.

Executive Decision Makers
Daniel VP Franchise OpsCamila Sr. Dir Finance
What would they try to do?Review the output of bulk edits. Take the scenario into an MBR or IC submission and defend it in under 60 seconds.Audit what assumptions a rule rests on. Validate projections are financially credible enough to approve an IC reserve request.
What frustrates them?Wall of numbers with no narrative. "Adjust +10%" not "This closes 2.3% of the gap." No ABP variance. No export for IC.No audit trail per rule. Corner badges cryptic. "Was X" shows vs. session baseline, not vs. target. No confidence indicators.
What's missing?Plain-English summary. ABP/target variance column. Read-only review mode. One-click IC export with assumptions.Rule justification field. Confidence indicators. Exportable audit log. Sensitivity flags. "Vs. ABP" column.
Operational Managers
Marco Dir Franchise Ops
What would they try to do?Open the panel mid-meeting, quickly adjust projections (+2% Distribution for Fanta at Asda), stack a few rules, save, and move on before the activation window closes.
What frustrates them?5-step wizard = 8+ interactions for a simple change. Panel auto-closes after apply. No rule reuse. No mobile path. Historicals and projections look the same.
What's missing?Quick-apply templates (save/reuse). Keep-panel-open mode. Impact summary before closing. Mobile-responsive layout. Conditional formatting (RAG).
Analytical Power Users
Sarah Dir Media/IMXElena Sr. Dir Strategy
What would they try to do?Model what-if scenarios for media reallocation. Validate math respects adstock and diminishing returns. Compare side-by-side.Scenario modelling for annual plan — "shift portfolio weight from Sprite to Coke Zero across H2." Feed into LRP/ABP cycle.
What frustrates them?"Adjust +10%" is naive flat math for media with adstock/saturation. No scenario comparison. No methodology per driver.Only 4 months of time scope. No "shift from Brand A to B" action. Scenarios vanish on refresh. Driver hierarchy is tactical.
What's missing?Curve-aware adjustments. Scenario comparison view. Driver methodology card. Confidence bands. Cross-channel synergy flags.Multi-year time horizon. Portfolio rebalancing. Forward impact on next year. Named/shared scenario library. Brand equity overlay.

Cross-Persona Gap Matrix

Maps each identified gap to the personas it affects. A primary blocker (P) means the gap directly prevents that persona from completing their core task — they can't work around it. A secondary marker (S) means the gap creates friction but isn't the thing that stops them cold.

P Primary blocker — this gap directly prevents their core task S Secondary — relevant pain but not their top concern
Gap Theme Context Daniel Camila Marco Sarah Elena
Executive summary / narrativeThe panel shows numbers but never tells the story — no one-liner for an MBR or IC.PS
Assumption audit trailRules show "+10%" but never record the benchmark or data source behind it.PS
ABP / target variance view"Was X" compares to session baseline, not the plan — users need vs. ABP/target.PPSS
Speed / fewer interaction steps5-step wizard = 8+ clicks for a simple change; operators need ~3 clicks to stay in flow.P
Non-linear / curve-aware mathFlat "+10%" ignores adstock, saturation, and diminishing returns on media.P
Scenario comparisonCan only work one scenario at a time; no side-by-side or diff view.PS
Multi-year time horizon4-month scope makes the tool unusable for annual or long-range planning.P
Portfolio rebalancingNo "shift from Brand A to B" action — requires two separate manual edits.P
Confidence / sensitivity indicatorsAll adjusted cells look equally certain; no signal for high-risk or low-confidence drivers.SPP
Shared / persistent scenariosScenarios live in sessionStorage and vanish on refresh — can't name, share, or build on them.SP
Rule justification / rationaleNo field to record "why" a rule was applied — makes post-hoc audit impossible.SP
Quick-repeat / templatesCan't save/reuse a rule config; repeating across brands means re-configuring from scratch.P
IC export artifactNo shareable output (PDF/slide) — users rebuild scenarios in Excel/slides before IC.PP

Scope

This testing plan covers Quick Edit (Bulk Edit Side Panel) only. Other features — Scenario Management, Date Picker, Optimiser Constraints, Summary Page Enhancements — follow their own testing tracks and are not included here.

Testing Methodology

Three-tier research approach. Each tier builds on the one before it — synthetic first, then validate with real users at increasing fidelity.

TierMethodWhen to UseHow to Run
1 · Synthetic AI persona review agent (internal) Before any user-facing test. Catch gaps in assumptions.
  • Define 3+ personas grounded in real user data
  • Run each major design through personas before scheduling sessions
  • Ask: What would they do? What confuses them? What's missing?
  • Log synthetic predictions vs. actual — gap improves personas over time
2 · Unmoderated User Testing (ideal) or Email (send prototype + questions) Specific answerable questions, quick feedback. A/B, preference tests, labeling comprehension.
  • Task framing is everything — specific scenario, clear starting point, no leading language
  • 5–8 participants is usually enough to see patterns
  • Follow up with moderated if results are surprising or contradictory
3 · Moderated Scheduled call (Teams) Novel flow, high-stakes epic, alignment with value or priority needed. When unmoderated results leave an open question.
  • Task-based, not demo-based — give users a scenario and let them drive
  • Think-aloud protocol — ask them to narrate what they're seeing and expecting
  • Observer notes, not just recording — designate one person to capture quotes in real time

Quick Edit: Testing Status

TesterTitleTest TypeScheduledStatus
Maq Ndebele Simulating Growth & Revenue Manager, CCEP Moderated Thursday, July 9, 6am ET Complete
Max Wendler Simulating Growth & Revenue Manager, CCEP Moderated — To Be Scheduled

Hypotheses to Validate in Next Test

Based on the synthetic persona review, these are the design gaps most likely to surface in moderated sessions. Prioritised by expected impact.

Solve Now

Conditional formatting

Red/amber/green health indicators on cells so users instantly know if a number is on-track or off.

Solve Now

Variance-to-target column

Show "vs. ABP" delta inline. Not just "was X" but "plan was Y, now Z."

Solve Now

Rule rationale field

One free-text input on Apply: "Based on H1 DFR performance." Transforms audit trail instantly.

Solve Now

Impact summary on apply

Before panel closes: "YTG moves from 91.2 to 93.0 (+2.0%). Closes 1.4% of ABP gap."

Solve Next

Quick-apply templates

Save and reuse recent rule configs. Makes Marco's weekly routine viable at speed.

Solve Next

Scenario naming & persistence

Named, shareable scenarios beyond sessionStorage. Unlocks Elena's planning and Sarah's comparison needs.

Solve Next

Executive summary card

Auto-generated one-line narrative above the table: "This scenario shifts YTG by X, closing Z% of ABP gap."

Solve for Scale

Curve-aware adjustments

Non-linear math respecting adstock and diminishing returns for media channels.

Solve for Scale

Multi-year horizon & portfolio rebalancing

Extend beyond current quarter. "Shift from Brand A to B" as a first-class operation.

Moderated Session Design

When to use Novel flow, high-stakes epic, or when alignment with value/priority is needed. Protects for decisions that require the "why," not just the "what."

Session Structure (45 min)

  1. Context setting (5 min) — Brief on scenario: "You're preparing for the Monthly Business Review. A colleague applied these bulk edits yesterday."
  2. Task 1: Comprehension (10 min) — "Look at this table. What happened? Is the scenario on track?"
  3. Task 2: Action (15 min) — "Apply a +5% adjustment to Digital Media for Coca-Cola across Q3. Walk me through your thinking."
  4. Task 3: Validation (10 min) — "Would you sign off on this scenario for IC submission? What's missing?"
  5. Debrief (5 min) — "What would you change about this experience?"

Hypotheses to Validate

  • H1: Users cannot determine "on track vs. off track" from the current table without mental arithmetic
  • H2: The 5-step wizard creates friction that discourages repeated use
  • H3: Executives will not use the output for IC without a narrative summary
  • H4: Finance will block adoption without assumption transparency

Unmoderated Session Design

When to use Specific, bounded questions that need an answer faster than a scheduled session allows. Good for A/B tests, preference tests, comprehension checks, and labeling validation.

Task Script

  1. Task 1: "Find the YTG value for Coca-Cola Digital Media. Is it above or below the annual target?" (measures: can they find it? do they know if it's good?)
  2. Task 2: "Increase Digital Media spend by 5% for July and August only." (measures: time-on-task, error rate, abandonment)
  3. Task 3: "You just applied a rule. What changed? Summarize in one sentence." (measures: comprehension of post-apply state)
  4. Task 4: "Apply the same rule you just used, but for Sprite this time." (measures: discoverability of repeat/template flow)

Success Metrics

  • Task 2 completion in under 90 seconds (current estimate: 3+ minutes)
  • Task 3 accuracy: user can correctly state the direction of change
  • Task 4: fewer than 50% of users re-configure from scratch (indicates template need)
  • SUS score above 68 (current predicted baseline: 55–60)
📥
Downloadable Materials

The full working ledger (27 worked samples, blank fill-in sheet, auto-scoring scorecard, and round log) lives in the Excel workbook:

Prediction Ledger (.xlsx) Full Assessment (.docx)

The Loop

Three steps, repeated after each user test. The gap between prediction and reality is the learning signal.

1

Predict

Before a test, log one row per prediction — persona, a specific and falsifiable predicted behavior, and your confidence (High / Medium / Low).

2

Compare

After the test, fill Actual behavior, pick the Delta, and note the Persona update needed.

3

Version

End of each round: apply the updates, bump the persona version, and add a Round Log row. Accuracy climbs over time (54% → 75% → 89% in the worked samples).

Delta Types

Correct Underestimated Overestimated Wrong Axis Surprising
DeltaMeaningAction
CorrectReal users behaved as predictedNo change needed
UnderestimatedUsers did more, cared more, or were more capableAdd or strengthen the trait
OverestimatedUsers did less, cared less, or were less demandingSoften or lower the trait
Wrong AxisRight behavior, wrong reason or dimensionFix the "why" behind it
SurprisingUsers did something no persona predictedAdd a net-new trait

Worked Samples

A sample from the full 27-row ledger. Each row is a specific, falsifiable prediction logged before a test.

ID Persona Predicted Behavior Conf. Feature Actual Behavior Delta Persona Update
P-01 Daniel Can't restate the scenario in one line from the panel — asks an analyst for the "what this says" before MBR/IC. High Side Panel (CXP) All 3 exec reviewers asked an analyst to summarize before MBR; none self-served the story. Correct Confirmed — build the scenario-narrative headline.
P-05 Camila Won't sign off — can't see why +10% (no benchmark or data source attached). High Side Panel (CXP) Finance reviewer refused to approve a rule with no rationale attached — exactly as predicted. Correct Confirmed — rationale field is a gating requirement.
P-10 Marco Finds the 5-step wizard too slow for a simple +2% — wants a ~3-click path. High Side Panel (Market) 4 of 5 finished the wizard, but all 5 asked for a faster repeat path; 1 abandoned mid-flow. Overestimated Soften "abandons wizard" — completion is fine; real need is speed on repeat.
P-15 Sarah Distrusts the flat +10% because it ignores adstock and diminishing returns. High Side Panel (Market) Media tester immediately flagged that +10% ignores diminishing returns — verbatim. Correct Confirmed — curve-aware mode needed for media drivers.
P-16 Sarah Looks for a link to Curves and won't trust linear math without it. Medium Side Panel (Market) Wanted the response curve but didn't expect it inside this panel — fine with a link out. Wrong Axis Right need, wrong form — it's curve transparency, not in-panel integration. A link suffices.
P-20 Elena Finds the Jul–Oct horizon too short for annual / multi-year planning. High Side Panel (CXP) Said the 4-month window made the tool unusable for annual planning — strong reaction. Correct Confirmed — multi-year horizon required for strategy use.
P-23 Elena Finds the L1–L4 channel hierarchy too tactical vs. her portfolio / brand-combo lens. Medium Side Panel (CXP) Navigated the hierarchy but wanted a portfolio / brand-combo layer on top. Underestimated She needs more structure than predicted — add a portfolio grouping above the channel hierarchy.
P-25 Marco (none — unpredicted) — Side Panel (Market) Ops users tried to undo a bulk edit and expected edit history / rollback — no persona anticipated this. Surprising Add "expects undo / rollback" to Marco (likely all operators).
P-26 Camila (none — unpredicted) — Side Panel (CXP) Finance reviewer asked who else had edited the scenario (multi-user attribution) — unforeseen. Surprising Add "wants edit attribution — who changed what" to Camila.

Showing 9 of 27 entries. Full ledger with all predictions, scoring, and round tracking available in the Excel workbook.

Scoring

Accuracy = Correct ÷ (all predictions except Surprising). Surprising sits outside — those are discoveries, not misses.

Confidence is logged so you can catch over-confidence: a persona that's often High-confidence but wrong needs recalibrating, not just editing.

Example progression Round 1: 54% accuracy → Round 2: 75% → Round 3: 89%. Each real test makes the next synthetic review more accurate.
Fuelight 360 Classified – Confidential · Web is source of truth · Last updated: July 16, 2026

Persona Profiles

Executive Decision Makers
D

Daniel

VP, Franchise Operations
Senior decision-makerABP ownerIC submitter
"Make it executive-readable and I'll engage. I won't run the model myself — I care whether I can defend it in an MBR."
Routines
Monthly Business Review · OU Investment Committee · Weekly volume connect · Annual planning
Metrics
VolumeRevenue / NSRDollar shareSOVIW+ROI
Pain Points
  • Key trends buried in scorecards
  • Fund moves take 6–8 weeks
  • IC cases built on gut
Literacy
High business acumen, low analytical depth. Needs to trust without understanding every parameter.
C

Camila

Senior Director, Finance
IC gatekeeperE-rate ownerAdoption validator
"What assumptions does this rest on, and can I audit them? If I can't answer that, I can't sign off."
Routines
Annual DME allocation · IC validation · YTD/QTD/MTD reporting · Bottler JBP · E-rate reviews
Metrics
E-rateOINSRCreation:activationDME by channel
Pain Points
  • Assumption opacity blocks adoption
  • Creation:activation unmeasured
  • Bottler DFR ~70% visible
Literacy
Highest trust bar. Stress-tests assumptions and benchmarks. If Camila won't sign off, the IC won't move.
Operational Managers
M

Marco

Director, Franchise Operations
Core power userDFR ownerRuns tool weekly
"I've got a weekly connect in an hour. I need to know if I should move, and I need to move now."
Routines
Weekly volume connect · Monthly scorecard · Pre-execution go/no-go · Trigger-point activations · IC submissions
Metrics
VolumeW+ / HH pen.Dollar shareSOVIDFR ROINSR
Pain Points
  • Power BI takes 45 min to populate
  • Moving funds is "art and a conversation"
  • No post-mortem framework
Literacy
Moderate. Operational, not analytical. Notices instantly if outputs contradict market reality.
Analytical Power Users
S

Sarah

Director, Media / IMX
Highest data literacyMethodology hawk
"I'll push back if the methodology isn't clear. Show me what data this uses and what's missing."
Routines
In-year media reallocation · Media IC cases · Channel mix planning · Reach/response-curve analysis
Metrics
Channel ROIReach & freq.AdstockBrand equitySynergy
Pain Points
  • Agency models annual, "directional"
  • No cross-channel visibility
  • Brand equity absent
Literacy
Analytically precise. Challenges adstock assumptions and data coverage gaps.
E

Elena

Senior Director, Strategy
LRP/ABP ownerPortfolio trade-offs
"Does this change what goes into the annual plan, or does it just explain what already happened?"
Routines
Annual & long-range planning · Brand/country prioritisation · Cross-functional alignment · Scenario planning
Metrics
Portfolio ROIBrand healthLT vs. ST valueNSR / OI at plan
Pain Points
  • Fuelight explains the past only
  • Short-term ROI vs. brand health
  • Cross-functional alignment manual
Literacy
Strategic. Thinks in planning cycles and portfolio trade-offs. Asks whether outputs are forward-looking.
